title: Nutrient Status Awareness Check
description: Reflect on signs and symptoms that may suggest nutrient insufficiencies affecting your mental health. This is not a diagnostic tool — it helps you identify areas to discuss with your healthcare provider.
instructions: Over the past month, how often have you experienced the following?

questions.text: Low energy or fatigue not explained by sleep problems
Muscle cramps, twitches, or restless legs
Low mood that doesn't fully respond to other treatments
Poor wound healing or frequent infections
Numbness or tingling in hands or feet
Bone or joint pain without clear cause
Difficulty sleeping despite good sleep hygiene

bands.label: Few Nutrient-Related Concerns
bands.description: You report few symptoms commonly associated with nutrient insufficiencies. Continue maintaining a balanced diet. This course will help you understand which nutrients are most important for mental health and how to ensure you're getting enough.

bands.label: Some Potential Nutrient Concerns
bands.description: You're experiencing some symptoms that can be associated with nutrient insufficiencies. Research shows that deficiencies in vitamin D, B vitamins, magnesium, omega-3s, and zinc can affect mental health. Consider discussing baseline nutrient testing with your healthcare provider.

bands.label: Notable Nutrient-Related Symptoms
bands.description: Multiple symptoms suggest your nutrient status may warrant attention. Studies show that correcting deficiencies in key nutrients can significantly improve mental health outcomes. We recommend discussing comprehensive nutrient testing with your healthcare provider.

bands.label: Significant Symptoms — See Your Provider
bands.description: You're experiencing frequent symptoms that may reflect nutrient insufficiencies or other medical conditions. Please discuss these symptoms with your healthcare provider for proper evaluation and testing. Do not self-treat with high-dose supplements without medical guidance.

disclaimer: This is an educational awareness tool, not a medical diagnostic. Many of these symptoms can have multiple causes unrelated to nutrition. Always consult a healthcare provider before starting supplements, especially if you take medications. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
